commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `claimed` command-ledger state for dispatch-bearing comment-router commands, writes that claim before acknowledgements and dispatch, and adds focused tests for claim suppression and final ledger upgrades.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence current-main path: dispatch-bearing commands execute before the ledger is appended and written, so a failure after dispatch but before the final write can replay the same command version. I did not run a destructive live dispatch.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed for the missing real behavior proof and the intentional pre-dispatch crash-window tradeoff; there is no narrow repair marker to dispatch yet.

target.title: Claim command dispatches before execution
target.body:
## Summary

- add a durable `claimed` command-ledger state for dispatch-bearing comment-router commands
- write that claim after mutation/capacity preflight and before ack comments or worker/review dispatches can run
- keep the normal completion ledger update after command side effects finish, upgrading `claimed` entries to `executed` or `waiting`

## Root cause

The comment router previously executed ready commands before appending them to `results/comment-router.json`. A command that emitted a `repository_dispatch` or workflow dispatch could therefore be replayed if the process failed before the final ledger write.

## Why it matters

The local ledger is the durable idempotency boundary for maintainer commands, while GitHub dispatch APIs own the external side effect. Claiming dispatch-bearing command versions before emitting external work keeps retry runs from launching duplicate review/repair workers after a later failure.

## Validation

- `pnpm run build:repair`
- `/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin/node --test test/repair/comment-router-utils.test.ts test/repair/comment-router-core.test.ts`
- `PATH=/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/bin:$PATH pnpm run test:repair`
- `PATH=/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/bin:$PATH pnpm run lint:repair`
- `PATH=/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/bin:$PATH pnpm run lint:scripts`
- `PATH=/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/bin:$PATH pnpm run format:check`
- `PATH=/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/bin:$PATH pnpm run check:active-surface`
- `PATH=/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:/Users/alexgild/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/bin:$PATH pnpm run check:limits`

## Maintainer note

This makes dispatch-bearing commands at-most-once across crashes by treating the pre-dispatch claim as terminal for replay suppression. If automatic recovery for the small pre-dispatch crash window is important, that can be added separately as an explicit stale-claim recovery path rather than replaying commands by default.
target.author: ag-linden
